summary.en: A gritty, creative hub and the city's cultural heart, this is a sprawling canvas of murals, independent galleries, and repurposed warehouses where locals come to drink and browse.
This is Old Vegas, the city's original core, where historic bars, serious steakhouses, and innovative music venues thrive in the shadow of classic neon.
A sprawling, multi-mile corridor of strip malls on Spring Mountain Road packed with some of the best and most authentic Asian cuisine in the country.
Centered around the UNLV campus, this area offers a glimpse into a more normal, lived-in Las Vegas, with a mix of student life, old-school businesses, and hidden gems.
Largely residential, this area hides some of the city's most acclaimed culinary institutions, offering sophisticated food and drink without the resort price tags.
Navigate the mega-resorts not as a tourist, but as a discoverer, seeking out the speakeasies, high-quality restaurants, and unique vantage points hidden in plain sight.

description.en: A whimsical, dimly lit cocktail bar with a vintage-heavy interior known for its unusual and creative seasonal cocktails.
Considered by many to be the best brewpub in the city, this spacious spot is dedicated to craft beer and atomic-age theming.
A legendary, no-frills dive bar famous for its loud music, karaoke nights, and an unapologetically old-school Vegas atmosphere.
The undisputed heart of the Arts District dining scene, serving seasonal Italian fare with house-made pasta in a bustling, chic-industrial space.
On the first Friday of the month, the neighborhood transforms into a massive block party with open galleries, street performers, and food trucks.
The oldest freestanding bar in Las Vegas, where locals once gathered on the roof to watch atomic bomb tests in the desert.
A true speakeasy hidden inside Commonwealth bar, offering an intimate experience with meticulously crafted cocktails and a strict set of rules.
A modern throwback to the glamorous steakhouses of vintage Vegas, located in the basement of the Circa resort.
Housed in the iconic glass dome of the Plaza Hotel, this steakhouse is a quintessential Old Vegas setting for a classic dinner.
Housed in a former federal courthouse, this immersive museum offers a fascinating look at the history of organized crime in America.
An outdoor 'boneyard' where retired neon signs from Las Vegas's past go to rest, offering a magical journey through the city's design history.
An open-air shopping and dining complex built from repurposed shipping containers, featuring a giant, fire-breathing praying mantis sculpture.

name.en: Màs Por Favor Taqueria Y Tequila

description.en: A vibrant taco shop that acts as a brilliant front for a hidden, cavelike, candlelit tequila and mezcal speakeasy.
A transportive tiki bar that commits fully to the theme, with waterfalls, talking skeletons, and a star-studded ceiling.
One of the most celebrated off-Strip restaurants, serving a creative, hearth-cooked menu that blends American influences with global flavors.
A perpetually busy spot famous for its hand-pulled noodles and rich, savory broths in a casual, no-frills setting.
An energetic and popular spot for Japanese barbecue (yakiniku), where you grill your own high-quality meats at your table.
The main attraction here is the act of discovery; park and walk a section of Spring Mountain Road to find specialty markets, dessert shops, and hidden restaurants.
A beloved local dive that combines the ethos of a no-nonsense bar with fantastic, Detroit-style pizza.
A true dive in every sense of the word, this is a place for cheap drinks and colorful characters—a genuine slice of old Vegas life.
A classic, old-school Italian-American joint complete with red-checkered tablecloths, live music, and a free limo service from the Strip.
A massive museum dedicated to the history of pinball where you can play hundreds of beautifully restored machines.
This Streamline Moderne movie palace is one of the most significant historic buildings in Las Vegas, a landmark of the neighborhood.
This street is a destination in itself, a main artery connecting Downtown to the University lined with dive bars, record shops, and eateries.
An unassuming building with an unmarked door that hides a temple to classic cocktails, with a menu organized by era.
A full-on mob-themed Italian restaurant where you enter through a secret door for a fun, theatrical experience with hearty food.
An independent institution that has been serving classic Italian food to celebrities and locals for over 40 years.
The experience is about making the journey to acclaimed restaurants and seeing the quiet, residential side of Las Vegas most tourists never encounter.
Tucked away behind a donut shop in the Aria, this speakeasy offers a sophisticated, music-filled escape from the casino floor.
Considered the best steakhouse in the city by many local foodies, this is a carnivorous celebration of quality and creativity.
A truly high-end Chinese restaurant with an opulent, jewel-box-like room and legendary Peking duck.
A colossal, world-changing piece of architecture whose ever-changing exosphere offers incredible, futuristic photo opportunities.

name.en: Las Vegas Grand Prix Circuit Walk

description.en: On a non-race day, trace the Formula 1 circuit on foot to understand the massive scale of this urban race course.
Marking the official entrance to the City of Las Vegas, these 80-foot-tall steel arches offer a modern bookend to the classic 'Welcome' sign.

prompt.en: Find a piece of street art that tells a story and capture it in a photo.
Take a selfie with a sculpture or public art installation that inspires you.
Find an interesting texture on a wall—peeling paint, old bricks, or graffiti—and take a close-up shot.
Photograph your coffee or cocktail against a backdrop that screams 'Arts District'.
Take a photo looking into an art gallery from the street, capturing the art within.
Find a building that is clearly a repurposed warehouse or old garage and photograph its most interesting feature.
Discreetly capture a photo that showcases the unique, independent fashion style of a local.
Use the bright Vegas sun to create a photo of interesting shadows cast by a building or sculpture.
Find a word or phrase in a piece of graffiti and make it the subject of your photo.
Take a photo that is dominated by one single, vibrant color you find in a mural.
Capture a reflection of a mural in a puddle, a car window, or a shopfront.
Pose thoughtfully in front of a large-scale mural and have a friend take your picture.
Photograph a unique, non-corporate, handmade, or vintage sign for a local business.
Capture the vibe of a bustling outdoor patio at a local bar or cafe.
Find an antique or vintage shop and photograph the most unusual object you can see through the window.
Capture a photo of a classic neon sign, focusing on its vibrant colors and unique design.
Take a selfie with a vintage casino or historic building in the background.
Frame a shot that shows a historic, old building next to a modern one.
Take a photo that captures the bustling, chaotic energy of Fremont Street.
Find and photograph a historical plaque or sign that tells a story about the area.
Photograph the entrance to a bar that looks like it has been there for decades.
Respectfully take a photo of a street performer or artist in action (and be sure to tip!).
Take an artistic photo of a classic cocktail at a vintage-style bar.
Take a photo looking straight up at the Fremont Street Experience canopy or a tall historic building.
After dark, capture the reflection of neon lights in a puddle or a window.
Find a doorway that looks mysterious or like it leads to a hidden place and take a picture.
Find a piece of public art or a statue that represents the spirit of Downtown and pose with it.
Find an interesting architectural detail on one of the older buildings and get a close-up shot.
Photograph the classic, elegant sign or entrance of a downtown steakhouse.
At dusk, take a long exposure shot of traffic moving down Fremont or Las Vegas Boulevard.
Take a photo of a dish you've never tried before.
Photograph the most interesting or colorful storefront you can find in a strip mall.
If you're eating noodles, get a dramatic shot of them being pulled from the bowl.
Go into an Asian market and take a photo of the most unusual snack or product you can find.
Find a sign with beautiful calligraphy or characters you don't recognize and capture its design.
Take a selfie with an elaborate tiki drink or in front of tropical-themed decor.
Find a restaurant with an open kitchen or window and capture the chefs at work.
Photograph a building that has traditional Asian architectural elements.
Find a bakery or dessert shop and photograph their most colorful creation.
Take a picture of a restaurant that looks very plain on the outside but is packed with people inside.
If you're at a BBQ spot, capture a dynamic photo of food sizzling on a grill.
Take a photo of a menu item with a name that sounds intriguing or mysterious.
Take a wide shot of one of the bustling shopping plazas, capturing multiple storefronts.
Find a business that looks like it might be hiding something else inside and photograph its entrance.
Find and photograph a pair of guardian lion (or 'foo dog') statues.
Capture the exterior of a classic, no-frills dive bar.
Photograph a restaurant that looks like it hasn't changed its decor in 30 years.
Take a photo that captures the student vibe near the UNLV campus.
Find a building with mid-century or Streamline Moderne architectural style and take a picture.
Take a selfie reflected in the glass of a pinball machine or arcade game.
Take a photo that you feel represents the 'lived-in', non-touristy feel of the area.
Find a record store or indie shop and photograph its cool storefront.
Get a mouth-watering shot of a slice of pizza from a local joint.
Capture a wide shot of a main street like Maryland Parkway or Charleston Blvd, showing the mix of businesses.
Find a business sign that looks like it's from the 70s or 80s and snap a photo.
Photograph a place that is clearly a favorite hangout for locals.
Find an Italian restaurant with red-checkered tablecloths and capture the classic ambiance.
Take an action shot of a classic arcade game being played.
Find a quiet residential street and take a photo that shows what normal life in Vegas looks like.
Take a selfie in front of a place that is refreshingly free of tourist gloss.
Photograph a restaurant or bar with a very unassuming or unmarked entrance.
Take a photo that shows the contrast of a cool, destination restaurant located in a normal, suburban-style neighborhood.
Capture a photo of a beautifully crafted classic cocktail in a dark, moody bar.
Find a restaurant that has a timeless, 'old-world' or 'power-broker' feel and photograph its entrance.
Take a selfie in a restaurant with a strong theme, like a mob speakeasy.
Take a photo from your car or rideshare that shows you're on a journey to a specific culinary destination.
Capture an image that conveys why a local would choose this spot over a Strip restaurant.
Find a place with dramatic, red-lit interiors and capture the clandestine atmosphere.
Photograph a menu that advertises a happy hour or special that seems too good to be true.
Take a photo of your group clinking glasses at a timeless, classic restaurant or bar.
Find an entrance that requires you to know a secret or pass a test and photograph it.
Photograph a place that has been a local institution for decades.
Get a dramatic, close-up shot of a perfectly cooked steak or other signature dish.
Take a selfie where you look like a discerning local who knows all the best spots.
Capture an image that highlights the lack of pretense at a high-quality, off-strip spot.
Find a speakeasy or hidden lounge and photograph its unassuming public-facing entrance.
Take a photo of a modern architectural marvel like the Sphere or the Gateway Arches from a unique angle.
Find a quiet, secluded spot within a bustling casino (like a garden or lounge) and capture the tranquility.
Photograph a stunningly opulent or luxurious dining room or hotel lobby.
Take a selfie in a location that makes you look like a high-end connoisseur.
From a pedestrian bridge or upper floor, take a photo looking down on the Strip.
Capture a beautifully plated dish at a high-end restaurant.
Take a photo at night that looks like it could be from a science fiction movie.
Stand on a part of the Grand Prix circuit and take a photo from a race car driver's point of view.
Take a photo that focuses on a detail that has nothing to do with gambling—art, nature, or design.
Shoot straight up at a tall, modern building to create an abstract architectural photo.
Find a pocket of nature or a garden inside a resort and photograph it.
Photograph a dramatic open kitchen or a unique food presentation.
Capture the reflection of the Strip's lights on a modern, glass-covered building.
Take a selfie in a spot on the Strip where you are surprisingly, peacefully alone.
